channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: In Albania, anger grows against the government for supporting a Kushner-linked luxury resort
item.description: Albania's government has given preliminary approval to plans for the luxury resort along a stretch of coastline, prompting daily protests and legal challenges by environmental groups.
item.content:encoded:
Albania's government has given preliminary approval to plans for the luxury resort along a stretch of coastline, prompting daily protests and legal challenges by environmental groups.

(Image credit: Vlasov Sulaj)
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Trump further guts Education Dept. by shifting oversight of special ed, civil rights
item.description: The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.
item.content:encoded:
The moves to the federal departments of Health and Human Services and Justice, respectively, would further dismantle an agency that President Donald Trump has vowed to close.

(Image credit: Al Drago)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: 'The Lost Founder' profiles a brilliant lawyer who helped craft the Constitution
item.description: Jesse Wegman's book tells the story of James Wilson, a largely forgotten founding father who lived a colorful life and died as a Supreme Court justice on the run from the law and creditors.
item.content:encoded: Jesse Wegman's book tells the story of James Wilson, a largely forgotten founding father who lived a colorful life and died as a Supreme Court justice on the run from the law and creditors.
item.dc:creator: Dave Davies

item.title: Live with a partner? You may be sharing more microbes than you think
item.description: A large study finds you may share about a quarter of your oral and gut microbes with the people you live with. Should you worry? We asked the experts.
item.content:encoded:
A large study finds you may share about a quarter of your oral and gut microbes with the people you live with. Should you worry? We asked the experts.

(Image credit: Benambot/iStockphoto)
item.dc:creator: Joseph Kim

item.title: The World Cup reminds us that the way to a visitor's heart ... is through their stomach
item.description: The action inside the stadiums isn't the only thing capturing the attention of international visitors. Turns out, they're discovering the delights of Buc-ee's, Waffle House, Wawa and free soda refills.
item.content:encoded:
The action inside the stadiums isn't the only thing capturing the attention of international visitors. Turns out, they're discovering the delights of Buc-ee's, Waffle House, Wawa and free soda refills.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: The war with Iran is making oil changes pricier. And a deal won't solve it
item.description: The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. may be the world's biggest producer of crude oil, but that's not the case for motor oil. The cost of lubricants is soaring, and even a tentative deal to end the war won't solve the problem.

(Image credit: Sergio Flores)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Pop albums are drowning in 'narrative.' What happens when we go in cold?
item.description: New albums by Lizzo and the rising artist Imani Imani are both "event" records — but one arrives with arguably too much backstory, the other with almost none.
item.content:encoded:
New albums by Lizzo and the rising artist Imani Imani are both "event" records — but one arrives with arguably too much backstory, the other with almost none.

(Image credit: Lizzo by Jason Renaud / Imani Imani courtesy of pgLang)
item.dc:creator: Sheldon Pearce

item.title: Survey confirms the struggle of working parents: 'No way to be two things at once'
item.description: A new Pew survey finds many working parents feel they cannot give 100% at either work or home. Benefits like paid sick leave and more affordable childcare could help.
item.content:encoded:
A new Pew survey finds many working parents feel they cannot give 100% at either work or home. Benefits like paid sick leave and more affordable childcare could help.

(Image credit: Cliff Jette for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: How Israel could complicate Iran peace negotiations. And, World Cup highlights
item.description: Israel has been sidelined in the agreement between the U.S. and Iran. It could spoil peace negotiations. And, it's been a thrilling start to the World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
Israel has been sidelined in the agreement between the U.S. and Iran. It could spoil peace negotiations. And, it's been a thrilling start to the World Cup.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Israeli ambassador to U.S. says Israel is 'not going to withdraw from South Lebanon'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Israel's ambassador to the U.S. Michael Leiter about the peace deal the Trump administration says it's made with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Israel's ambassador to the U.S. Michael Leiter about the peace deal the Trump administration says it's made with Iran.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
